title: Unify OpenAI Responses v8: seed routing + context-tag metadata passthrough
preview: Summary: Add a compatibility layer for the incoming Responses v8 variant that preserves deterministic hints (seed/rng flags), forwards client metadata tags intact, and stabilizes streaming chunk boundaries for tool/function call reassembly. This PR centralizes the mapping logic so that other adapters (SDKs, private ...
text:
Summary: Add a compatibility layer for the incoming Responses v8 variant that preserves deterministic hints (seed/rng flags), forwards client metadata tags intact, and stabilizes streaming chunk boundaries for tool/function call reassembly. This PR centralizes the mapping logic so that other adapters (SDKs, private installer) can reuse the same translation rules. Motivation: Customers are adopting the new Responses v8 semantics (explicit seed + context-tag fields). Without a consistent mapping we see reproducibility regressions and toolcall reassembly bugs in streaming. This change reduces conversion drift and eliminates a class of flakiness observed in production canaries.

What this changes (high-level):
- Add responses-v8 adapter in pkg/openai/compat that maps v8 flags to internal RNG hints (seed-channel + deterministic-level).
- Preserve top-level client metadata (user_metadata, c...

title: Structured retry metadata and scoped idempotency for streamed tool calls
preview: Summary: Add a small, structured retry metadata header and an explicit idempotency-scoping mechanism to the OpenAI compatibility shim and streaming runtime so callers and downstream routers can make deterministic retry decisions for streamed tool/function calls. This clarifies Retry-After semantics and prevents idem...
text:
Summary: Add a small, structured retry metadata header and an explicit idempotency-scoping mechanism to the OpenAI compatibility shim and streaming runtime so callers and downstream routers can make deterministic retry decisions for streamed tool/function calls. This clarifies Retry-After semantics and prevents idempotency key collisions across multiplexed streaming fragments.

Motivation: Customers observed inconsistent retry behavior for long-lived streamed tool calls (tool/function invocations) when proxies and CDNs normalized Retry-After or when clients used coarse idempotency keys across multiple logical tool invocations in the same session. The current behavior led to duplicate tool executions or silent rate limit loops for partial streams. This PR provides: 1) a compact structured header with deterministic retry window data, 2) an explicit idempotency_scope header to partition ...

title: Self-healing traffic gate with prognostic rollback scheduler
preview: Motivation: We observed incident patterns where a small regression in an A/B arm degraded user experience slowly and only surfaced after sustained traffic. This PR adds a prognostic rollback scheduler and a self-healing traffic gate that combines short-horizon telemetry forecasting, a conservative anomaly scorer, an...
text: Motivation: We observed incident patterns where a small regression in an A/B arm degraded user experience slowly and only surfaced after sustained traffic. This PR adds a prognostic rollback scheduler and a self-healing traffic gate that combines short-horizon telemetry forecasting, a conservative anomaly scorer, and lightweight replay/bisect workflows so the system can proactively hold or revert risky promotes before widespread impact. Summary of changes: implemented a prognostic score pipeline that consumes recent SLO/time-series windows and outputs a short-term risk forecast; added TrafficGate controller that enforces holdpoints when forecasted risk crosses policy thresholds; built a rescue-oracle that can run fast replay of recent 1% samples in a sandbox to validate rollback decisions; added operator-visible "rescue" and "replay" actions to Redwood Console so on-call can review di...

title: per-request graceful-degradation: kernel compatibility shim + precision-hint API
preview: Motivation: We continue to see tail latency regressions in mixed-precision kernels when models with large kv-cache footprints are routed through the 'fast' execution path. This PR introduces a lightweight compatibility shim and a per-request 'precision hint' header that lets the router/scheduler choose a conservativ...
text:
Motivation: We continue to see tail latency regressions in mixed-precision kernels when models with large kv-cache footprints are routed through the 'fast' execution path. This PR introduces a lightweight compatibility shim and a per-request 'precision hint' header that lets the router/scheduler choose a conservative execution mode for requests that prioritize correctness over absolute token throughput.

What this PR does (single-string description): Adds a kernel-compat shim layer in the runtime that detects binary incompatibility or quantization sensitivity for a model+request combination and exposes a low-overhead boolean that the scheduler uses to avoid aggressive fusion/quantization fast-paths; implements an opt-in HTTP header (X-Redwood-Precision-Hint: prefer-correct | prefer-fast | neutral) and SDK flags that map to the same hint; extends continuous-batching logic to respect pe...

title: Probabilistic length cohorter with pad-aware scheduling and KV-prefill heuristics
preview: Motivation: long-tail sequence length variance continues to drive excessive padding and suboptimal kernel utilization in our continuous batching pipeline. Simple bucketing reduces padding but creates brittle boundaries and poor utilization when per-slot latency SLOs are tight. This PR introduces a probabilistic leng...
text:
Motivation: long-tail sequence length variance continues to drive excessive padding and suboptimal kernel utilization in our continuous batching pipeline. Simple bucketing reduces padding but creates brittle boundaries and poor utilization when per-slot latency SLOs are tight. This PR introduces a probabilistic length cohorter that (1) forms soft cohorts by sampling length-affinity probabilities instead of hard buckets, (2) attaches a pad-cost estimate to each pending request using a small learned linear cost model, and (3) integrates KV-prefill priority to bias cohorts toward requests that benefit from warm KV caches. The goal is to reduce aggregate padding waste while keeping P95 latency within SLO and increasing kernel lane alignment for improved throughput.

Approach: the cohorter computes a length-affinity vector for each request based on requested max_tokens, recent sequence len...

title: Epochal hotpath bisector and phase-stress harness for p95 reproduction
preview: Motivation: We've seen recurring intermittent p95 regressions across long-running multi-epoch workloads where the signal only appears after many warmup cycles. Root-cause is often a narrow commit window interacting with kernel scheduler behaviour and warmup timing. This PR introduces two complementary tools: (1) an ...
text:
Motivation: We've seen recurring intermittent p95 regressions across long-running multi-epoch workloads where the signal only appears after many warmup cycles. Root-cause is often a narrow commit window interacting with kernel scheduler behaviour and warmup timing. This PR introduces two complementary tools: (1) an epochal hotpath bisector that runs compact workload epochs across commit ranges to quickly narrow offending commits, and (2) a phase-stress harness that injects controlled scheduling/jitter/prefetch delays into the runtime to reliably amplify the rare p95 event so it becomes reproducible in CI/local benches.

What this change does: Implements a bisector CLI that (a) shards an epochal workload into deterministic micro-epochs, (b) runs them across a git commit range with parallel workers and conservative resource isolation, and (c) aggregates per-epoch phase-bucket histograms...

title: introduce adaptive sensitivity scheduler for canary detectors
preview: Motivation: long-running canary evaluators are sensitive to traffic volatility and changing baseline variance; fixed detection thresholds either generate noisy false positives during churn or miss subtle regressions when traffic is stable. This PR implements an Adaptive Sensitivity Scheduler (ASS) that continuously ...
text:
Motivation: long-running canary evaluators are sensitive to traffic volatility and changing baseline variance; fixed detection thresholds either generate noisy false positives during churn or miss subtle regressions when traffic is stable. This PR implements an Adaptive Sensitivity Scheduler (ASS) that continuously tunes detector sensitivity based on short-term traffic variance, cohort churn, and historical false-positive rate.

What it does (high level): introduces a scheduler that adjusts detector thresholds and aggregation windows at runtime. The scheduler observes three signals: instantaneous request-rate volatility, percentile drift across 1/5/15-minute windows, and the detector's empirical false-positive count. Using a lightweight Bayesian update, it adapts the detection sensitivity multiplier and the sample window size with safety bounds to avoid oscillation. Integrations added...

title: Add quantile-weighted, phase-aware throughput fence and severity scoring
preview: Problem: Existing canary detectors surface aggregate latency shifts but often miss multi-phase regressions where different traffic slices degrade at different times. We need a detector that (1) understands traffic phase boundaries (time-of-day, model warmup phases, routing slices) and (2) produces a single severity ...
text:
Problem: Existing canary detectors surface aggregate latency shifts but often miss multi-phase regressions where different traffic slices degrade at different times. We need a detector that (1) understands traffic phase boundaries (time-of-day, model warmup phases, routing slices) and (2) produces a single severity score that reflects both quantile skew and the persistence of the shift so rollout guards can make informed decisions.

This PR introduces a quantile-weighted, phase-aware throughput fence and a severity scoring function that folds into the canary evaluator pipeline. The detector computes quantile deltas per phase window and applies an exponential decay weighting across recent windows to emphasize sustained regressions while suppressing short-lived bursts. The severity score is a monotonic mapping of weighted-quantile deltas to [0,100] and is used by policy engines to trigg...

title: Unify latency-aware retry profiles and add lightweight StreamOperator shim across Py/TS/Go SDKs
preview: Motivation: Customers running inference across unstable networks (mobile/edge) see bursty p95/p99 spikes that cause suboptimal retry decisions (either aggressive retries causing overload or conservative backoffs hurting latency). This PR introduces a latency-aware retry profile that adapts backoff floors using short...
text: Motivation: Customers running inference across unstable networks (mobile/edge) see bursty p95/p99 spikes that cause suboptimal retry decisions (either aggressive retries causing overload or conservative backoffs hurting latency). This PR introduces a latency-aware retry profile that adapts backoff floors using short-term p95 latency windows and exposes a small StreamOperator shim to normalize streaming cancelation / graceful-drain semantics across Python, TypeScript, and Go SDKs. Summary of changes: 1) Add network-aware retry profiles (LatencyAwarePolicy) and a lightweight metrics window used by the retry controller. 2) Introduce StreamOperator: a minimal cross-language adapter that provides Pause/Resume/Abort semantics and a consistent onClose reason propagation so SDKs behave identically when servers send partial error frames or mid-stream reauth redirects. 3) Ergonomic wrappers: Py...

title: split-heavy-integration-tests-add-incremental-test-runner-and-dep-gate
preview: Motivation: Our nightly and PR CI pipelines are dominated by a small set of heavy integration tests that run even when irrelevant files change. This PR: 1) splits heavy integration tests into an explicit heavy-integration job that only runs when infra/runtime or integration test files change (pattern: tests/integrat...
text: Motivation: Our nightly and PR CI pipelines are dominated by a small set of heavy integration tests that run even when irrelevant files change. This PR: 1) splits heavy integration tests into an explicit heavy-integration job that only runs when infra/runtime or integration test files change (pattern: tests/integration/**, infra/**, runtime/**) 2) introduces an incremental test runner called 'fast-checker' which runs unit tests and a lightweight set of integration smoke tests for most PRs; it uses a file change heuristic to determine coverage 3) adds a dependency viability gate that runs a quick dependency-check step (build-profiler + dep-heuristic) on PRs that touch package manifests or lockfiles and fails the gating stage if a dependency update increases binary size or adds native toolchain requirements beyond configured thresholds 4) augments release automation to defer tag creatio...

title: Presubmit: introduce smart-delta change detector and release-quiesce adaptor
preview: Motivation: our presubmit pipeline is expensive and noisy for small documentation/typo changes and adds latency to fast patches. Separately, release automation occasionally collides with on-call hotfixes because we lack a short quiesce phase that gates non-critical deploys. This PR introduces two orthogonal improvem...
text:
Motivation: our presubmit pipeline is expensive and noisy for small documentation/typo changes and adds latency to fast patches. Separately, release automation occasionally collides with on-call hotfixes because we lack a short quiesce phase that gates non-critical deploys. This PR introduces two orthogonal improvements in a single, low-risk rollout: 1) a lightweight smart-delta detector (smart-delta) that classifies PRs into minimal-presubmit / full-presubmit lanes based on changed paths and simple AST heuristics; 2) a release-quiesce adaptor that allows the release pipeline to declaratively request a short quiesce window (default 5m) during which non-urgent merges are delayed until a safety window is cleared.

Summary of changes:
- new pkg/presubmit/smartdelta: change detector service + CLI used by CI to select presubmit profile
- presubmit-profiles.yaml: adds minimal, fast, and ful...

title: Introduce stage-aware merge prober and slimline tooling shim
preview: Context and motivation: Merges into main occasionally surface release-hook regressions or flaky lint/test failures that only appear in the full release smokeflow. This PR introduces a lightweight, stage-aware 'merge-prober' that runs a short, cost-controlled set of checks before allowing a squash/merge and refactors...
text:
Context and motivation: Merges into main occasionally surface release-hook regressions or flaky lint/test failures that only appear in the full release smokeflow. This PR introduces a lightweight, stage-aware 'merge-prober' that runs a short, cost-controlled set of checks before allowing a squash/merge and refactors the existing monolithic lint/test runner into a plugin adapter to make fast-path lanes pluggable and easier to maintain. The goal is to shorten merge feedback loops and reduce post-merge rollbacks without adding heavy CI latency.
Changes at a glance: 1) New merge-prober service and GH Actions job that performs staged checks (configurable by repo labels); 2) Refactor lint/test runner into a plugin interface, extract Node/Go/TS plugins; 3) Bump a handful of GitHub Actions and pin SHAs for reproducibility; 4) Remove legacy scripts in scripts/ci-legacy and migrate their small,...

title: Coordinate cross-SDK credential surface and streaming ergonomics
preview: Motivation: Multiple SDK teams (Python/TypeScript/Go) have drifted in credential APIs and streaming ergonomics: mismatch in session factory shapes, differing retry preset names, and inconsistent stream iterator behaviour caused duplicated adaptation logic in upstream services. This PR coordinates a minimal, backward...
text:
Motivation: Multiple SDK teams (Python/TypeScript/Go) have drifted in credential APIs and streaming ergonomics: mismatch in session factory shapes, differing retry preset names, and inconsistent stream iterator behaviour caused duplicated adaptation logic in upstream services. This PR coordinates a minimal, backwards-compatible convergence across the three SDKs by introducing a small cross-language contract (documented in sdk/README/SESSION.md), consolidating retry preset names, and adding a lightweight token-warmup hook for stream-first requests.

What this PR does (high level): 1) Introduces SessionFactory v2 surface (polyglot contract) and a thin compatibility shim on top of existing SessionFactory implementations to avoid mass migrations. 2) Adds a RetryProfile alias map so \"default\"/\"standard\"/\"balanced\" map to a canonical engine preset at runtime and surfaces the mapping i...

title: operator credential bakeoff: add KMS provenance proxy and RBACv2 residency sentinel
preview: Background and motivation: We routinely see two operational risks during maintenance windows and operator actions: 1) operator credentials getting reused across regions leading to ambiguous KMS telemetry, and 2) audit logs that lack strong, tamper-evident provenance tying a request to the exact KMS path used. This P...
text:
Background and motivation: We routinely see two operational risks during maintenance windows and operator actions: 1) operator credentials getting reused across regions leading to ambiguous KMS telemetry, and 2) audit logs that lack strong, tamper-evident provenance tying a request to the exact KMS path used. This PR introduces a lightweight, opt-in 'credential bakeoff' flow that 1) proxies KMS calls to capture provenance assertions, 2) records an immutable provenance token in the audit ledger, and 3) performs RBACv2 shadow-checks (residency sentinel) before approving cross-region operator actions. The goal is to enable safe testing of stricter policies without immediate enforcement.

What this PR does (high level):
- Add internal/kms/provenance_proxy: a sidecar-like HTTP proxy component that intercepts KMS calls from operator tooling, validates upstream KMS responses, and emits a sig...

title: add-invoke-slice-sampler-and-token-cascade-explainer
preview: Motivation: long-tail token cost and latency noise make it hard to correlate bursts to kernel warmup, prefix vs completion cost, and scheduling contention. This PR introduces two complementary observability primitives: (1) an Invoke Slice Sampler that slices invocations into time-aligned 'slices' to preserve bursty ...
text: Motivation: long-tail token cost and latency noise make it hard to correlate bursts to kernel warmup, prefix vs completion cost, and scheduling contention. This PR introduces two complementary observability primitives: (1) an Invoke Slice Sampler that slices invocations into time-aligned 'slices' to preserve bursty token-attribution while dramatically reducing cardinality and storage cost; and (2) a Token Cascade Explainer that attributes per-token cost/latency to a small set of causal contributors (prefix KV hits/misses, kernel warmup penalty, batching wait, and I/O stalls) and exposes an explainer panel in the console. Summary of changes: - New sampler: telemetry/ingest/slice_sampler.go adds a deterministic slice keyed by (tenant, route, invocation_bucket) and emits sampled span anchors plus compact slice digests. Sampler is opt-in via telemetry.config.slice_sampling.enabled=false|t...

title: shortburst-attention-fusion-and-slab-packing-for-short-sequence-latency
preview: Motivation: Many customer workloads at Redwood are dominated by short sequences (<64 tokens) where per-request overhead (kernel launch, KV cache management, micro-batch alignment) dominates token cost. This PR introduces a runtime path optimized for short-burst workloads: 1) an attention-fusion kernel variant that f...
text: Motivation: Many customer workloads at Redwood are dominated by short sequences (<64 tokens) where per-request overhead (kernel launch, KV cache management, micro-batch alignment) dominates token cost. This PR introduces a runtime path optimized for short-burst workloads: 1) an attention-fusion kernel variant that fuses small-sequence attention + rotary/ALiBi precompute to reduce kernel launches for sequences <= 64, 2) a token-slab packing allocator that packs multiple short requests into a single device slab with per-request offsets to avoid per-token KV allocations, and 3) a micro-continuous batching tweak that lowers the batching tick for short sequences while preserving overall throughput for long sequences. The goal is to reduce median latency for short requests without regressions for long-tail workloads. Changes: implemented fused_attention_short.cu and refactored attention dis...

title: triad-sdk-credential-window-and-stream-flow-refinements
preview: Motivation: customers reported sporadic 401s during long pull/stream sessions and awkward ergonomics for checking stream liveness. This PR introduces a lightweight credential 'sliding window' that normalizes prefetch timing across SDKs, plus two small stream ergonomics helpers (peek_end and wait_for_end) and instrum...
text: Motivation: customers reported sporadic 401s during long pull/stream sessions and awkward ergonomics for checking stream liveness. This PR introduces a lightweight credential 'sliding window' that normalizes prefetch timing across SDKs, plus two small stream ergonomics helpers (peek_end and wait_for_end) and instrumentation to correlate reauth events with stream traces. Changes span Python/TypeScript/Go SDKs and docs. What changed: 1) Credential sliding window: add a tunable lease_window and prefetch_margin to SDK credential providers so refresh is deterministic across clock skew and latency. Default profile keeps behavior identical but allows operators to reduce reauth jitter for long-lived streams. 2) Stream ergonomics: add Stream.peek_end() to poll if the upstream finished without consuming tokens, and Stream.wait_for_end(timeout) as a convenience awaitable. Implementations are ful...

title: Add hardware-timestamp tracepoints and synthetic-replay microbench for kernel latency and tile occupancy
preview: Motivation: pinpoint sub-kernel latency variance and per-tile occupancy skew seen during continuous batching on long sequences. This PR adds lightweight hardware-timestamp tracepoints around kernel launches, a small lock-free per-tile occupancy counter, and a deterministic synthetic-replay microbenchmark that replay...
text: Motivation: pinpoint sub-kernel latency variance and per-tile occupancy skew seen during continuous batching on long sequences. This PR adds lightweight hardware-timestamp tracepoints around kernel launches, a small lock-free per-tile occupancy counter, and a deterministic synthetic-replay microbenchmark that replays recorded request traces at controlled concurrency for stable profiling. Changes are gated behind runtime flags so there is zero overhead when disabled. High-level summary: 1) runtime/tracing/hw_timestamp_trace.h/.cc implements RAII-style trace scopes that capture wallclock and GPU hw timestamps when available and annotate the internal timeline with span ids, 2) runtime/scheduler/tile_counter.h/.cc introduces a cache-friendly per-tile occupancy counter sampled by the scheduler to record occupancy distributions, 3) tools/replay_bench parses a compressed request trace and re...

title: conditional eval fanout + weighted signal aggregator and CI gating
preview: Motivation: when a primary prompt-set check trips, we often need to run a set of targeted probes (e.g. hallucination prober, paraphrase-stability, token-saliency) to determine failure mode before blocking rollouts. This PR adds a conditional eval fanout layer and a weighted signal aggregator so CI can produce a sing...
text: Motivation: when a primary prompt-set check trips, we often need to run a set of targeted probes (e.g. hallucination prober, paraphrase-stability, token-saliency) to determine failure mode before blocking rollouts. This PR adds a conditional eval fanout layer and a weighted signal aggregator so CI can produce a single composite gating decision that reflects both severity and breadth of regressions. Design summary: - New runtime component eval_fanout orchestrates conditional parallel probes with a budget cap and backpressure to avoid blowing smoke-test windows. - WeightedSignalAggregator implements decay-weighted aggregation across cohorts and probes; weights are configurable per-linear-task and default to severity-first then coverage. - CiHook composite_slo_check integrates into existing eval CI: primary prompt-set runs as usual; on fail, fanout triggers configured probes; aggregator ...

title: introduce-cost-rules-engine-and-autotune-batching-defaults
preview: Summary: Add a Cost Rules Engine that automatically recommends and applies batching and KV-cache defaults per model and workload profile, exposes quantization profile hints for safe auto-application, and introduces a unit-economics estimator in the console and CLI. Motivation: customers routinely ask for hands-off c...
text: Summary: Add a Cost Rules Engine that automatically recommends and applies batching and KV-cache defaults per model and workload profile, exposes quantization profile hints for safe auto-application, and introduces a unit-economics estimator in the console and CLI. Motivation: customers routinely ask for hands-off cost tuning; operational teams want reproducible, audited defaults that reduce per-token costs without surprising latency regressions. This PR bundles runtime heuristics, a small rules DSL, console UI plumbing, and an opt-in rollout for auto-quantization. What changed (high level): - new runtime module redwood/serving/cost_rules.py with rules evaluation, telemetry hooks, and safe-apply gates - batcher tuning extension that selects default batch sizes based on latency SLOs and sequence-length histograms - KV cache policy defaults and prefix cache eligibility heuristics - quan...

title: Introduce presubmit-prober-lite and Oxide dep cleanser to reduce CI setup noise
preview: Context: The presubmit pipeline has grown noisy and slow due to full toolchain fetches, overly conservative devdep pinning, and repeated cold-start overheads in ephemeral CI runners. Motivation: Reduce pretest latency and flakiness by introducing a lightweight presubmit prober that warms an ephemeral 'shadow' artifa...
text: Context: The presubmit pipeline has grown noisy and slow due to full toolchain fetches, overly conservative devdep pinning, and repeated cold-start overheads in ephemeral CI runners. Motivation: Reduce pretest latency and flakiness by introducing a lightweight presubmit prober that warms an ephemeral 'shadow' artifact cache, a sparse-fetch wrapper to avoid pulling full toolchains for quick presubmit lanes, and a small dependency-cleanser (Oxide) that flags / optionally prunes devdeps causing large install graphs. This PR bundles a tooling + workflow refactor to give teams an opt-in fast lane for quick feedback and a hygiene tool to keep CI images and lockfiles lean. Summary of changes: - tools/presubmit-prober/: new Go binary (small, dependency-free) that probes common package registries, exercises cache layers, and warms a shadow cache on the runner prior to heavy installs. It exits ...

title: introduce-telemetry-correlation-hub-and-alert-prioritizer
preview: Motivation: Customers repeatedly request better correlation between token-level cost spikes, kernel/backpressure metrics, and the sparse logs/trace fragments we capture. This PR adds a Telemetry Correlation Hub: an index + lightweight merge pipeline that synthesizes compact span digests, token-cost fingerprints, and...
text: Motivation: Customers repeatedly request better correlation between token-level cost spikes, kernel/backpressure metrics, and the sparse logs/trace fragments we capture. This PR adds a Telemetry Correlation Hub: an index + lightweight merge pipeline that synthesizes compact span digests, token-cost fingerprints, and contextual log anchors to improve triage and to drive an alert prioritizer that reduces noisy SRE toil. Design summary: - Introduce correlation index (compact-correlation-key) derived from invocation signature + first-3-token hash + kernel-shape fingerprint. - Lightweight merge step at ingestion that joins sampled spans, cost-attribution rows, and log anchors into a compact "tracelet" document stored in the telemetry store. - Alert prioritizer component that computes a per-anomaly priority score (severity*cost-impact*entropy) and attaches a reason vector used by the consol...

title: Add pseudonymous token-cost summarizer and privacy-hash bridge
preview: Context/Motivation: We need per-invocation token cost visibility without retaining plaintext prompt or response tokens in observability backends. This PR introduces a privacy-first pipeline: (1) deterministic, salted token-hash bucketing at the edge; (2) a compact per-invocation cost summarizer that attributes per-b...
text: Context/Motivation: We need per-invocation token cost visibility without retaining plaintext prompt or response tokens in observability backends. This PR introduces a privacy-first pipeline: (1) deterministic, salted token-hash bucketing at the edge; (2) a compact per-invocation cost summarizer that attributes per-bucket token counts and tokenized-costs; (3) a bridge service that joins hashed token buckets with model cost profiles for rollups and dashboards. Goals: provide accurate cost attribution for SLOs and billing heuristics while ensuring PII/PHI is never logged or exported. Changes are designed to be reversible (config toggles) and auditable. Main changes: - collector: add pseudonymization middleware that applies a per-tenant salt and BLAKE3-based chunked hashing to token fragments, exposing only bucket_id and hash_prefix_size metadata (no token text). - summarizer: a streaming...

title: Ephemeral tenant shelf and circuit epoch guard
preview: Motivation: During a production incident last week we observed tenant recovery flapping when nodes with partially-drained KV caches rejoined. That flapping caused rapid circuit reopen/close cycles and triggered SLO alerts for the affected region. This PR introduces two coordinated features: 1) an ephemeral tenant-sh...
text: Motivation: During a production incident last week we observed tenant recovery flapping when nodes with partially-drained KV caches rejoined. That flapping caused rapid circuit reopen/close cycles and triggered SLO alerts for the affected region. This PR introduces two coordinated features: 1) an ephemeral tenant-shelf that temporarily places low-priority tenant requests into a short-lived queue while the system heals, and 2) a circuit-epoch guard which binds circuit state transitions to epoch windows to prevent rapid reopen oscillations. The design aims to reduce cascade reopens, smooth SLO enforcement during regional rejoin events, and provide clear telemetry for postmortem analysis. Summary of changes: - New TenantShelf component in serving/runtime to accept and hold low-priority requests with configurable shelf TTL and max depth. - EpochGuard added to circuit-breaker state machine...

title: SDKS: auth mosaic + streaming consolidation and ergonomic API polish
preview: Context: We observed subtle divergence in how Python/TypeScript/Go SDKs handled token refresh during long-lived streams and how retry presets influence stream resume characteristics. This caused two classes of production issues: (1) opaque reauth races that produced 401s during resume, and (2) inconsistent backpress...
text: Context: We observed subtle divergence in how Python/TypeScript/Go SDKs handled token refresh during long-lived streams and how retry presets influence stream resume characteristics. This caused two classes of production issues: (1) opaque reauth races that produced 401s during resume, and (2) inconsistent backpressure hints leading to latency spikes when batching heuristics changed across SDKs. This PR introduces a small cross-SDK surface (SessionSurface) and a compact "mosaic" of auth utilities to align behavior. Key goals: harmonize reauth handshake, provide ergonomic retry presets (latency-first and throughput-first), and expose minimal stream backpressure hints to SDK consumers. Changes: - Introduce SessionSurface (py) and SessionFactory (go) as a lightweight facade for long-lived sessions; exported helpers in ts for parity. - Add two retry preset bundles: latency_aware and throu...

title: coordinated-rate-aware-retry-and-stream-drain-trio-sdk-bump
preview: Context and motivation: We observed customer workloads where token quota bursts and rate limiting created poor tail latency and spurious retries across SDKs. Separately, deploys and autoscale events produced abrupt stream cancellations that surfaced as confusing errors in end-user apps. This PR implements a coordina...
text:
Context and motivation: We observed customer workloads where token quota bursts and rate limiting created poor tail latency and spurious retries across SDKs. Separately, deploys and autoscale events produced abrupt stream cancellations that surfaced as confusing errors in end-user apps. This PR implements a coordinated cross-SDK change: (1) rate-aware retry windows that scale backoff slopes and jitter based on observed success-rate and throttle signals, and (2) a graceful stream-drain/quiesce helper that allows long-lived streaming consumers to finish inflight messages before reconnecting. The goal is parity across Python, TypeScript, and Go SDKs with minimal ergonomics changes for integrators.

What changed (high level):
- Added "RateAwareRetryProfile" and default presets (conservative/throughput/aggressive). Profiles adjust maxRetryWindow, baseBackoff and jitter envelope based on re...

title: Add release dry-run workflow, lightweight lint-prober runner, and dependency impact scanner
preview: Context: Release windows have been a recurring source of risk — releases usually exercise cross-repo hooks (changelogs, tag signing, and artifact promotion) but we lacked a reliable preflight to validate those steps and the telemetry emitted during a release. This PR introduces three coordinated pieces: (1) a releas...
text: Context: Release windows have been a recurring source of risk — releases usually exercise cross-repo hooks (changelogs, tag signing, and artifact promotion) but we lacked a reliable preflight to validate those steps and the telemetry emitted during a release. This PR introduces three coordinated pieces: (1) a release-dryrun workflow that performs a full non-mutating rehearsal of release steps in CI and emits structured telemetry; (2) a lightweight lint-prober runner that can execute a minimal subset of presubmit linters quickly on changed files (target fast feedback for large monorepo touchpoints); and (3) a dependency impact scanner that maps changed dependency versions to a quick-risk score for release gating. Motivation: reduce release surprises, shorten the pre-merge feedback loop for lint failures on large changes, and flag high-risk dependency bumps before rollout. Changes are i...

title: kv: granular coalesce evictor and kernel-aware routing for spill reduction
preview: Context: We see recurrent small-fragment churn in tenant KV caches under mixed-generation workloads (short prompt inference + chat continuations). Frequent small-page evictions increase spill frequency and disk IO, which amplifies latency variance and costs. This PR implements a new eviction strategy (Granular Coale...
text:
Context: We see recurrent small-fragment churn in tenant KV caches under mixed-generation workloads (short prompt inference + chat continuations). Frequent small-page evictions increase spill frequency and disk IO, which amplifies latency variance and costs. This PR implements a new eviction strategy (Granular Coalesce Evictor, GCE) combined with kernel-aware routing signals to reduce unnecessary spills and improve tail latency for production workloads.

What this PR does: Implements GCE that: (1) detects clusters of small adjacent fragments and coalesces them in-memory before choosing to spill; (2) uses a hybrid scoring function that weights fragmentation impact, tenant priority, and an estimated kernel recompile/execution cost to prefer evicting entries that are cheaper to rehydrate; (3) introduces a burst-aware coalescer that defers full compaction under sustained IO pressure. Inte...

title: Budget Savant: Prefetch Heuristics + Quant Tier Manager
preview: Context: Large tenants with bursty tail traffic waste tokens and GPU cycles during cold KV-cache windows. Goal: introduce a tenant-scoped prefilling heuristic and a quantization-tier manager that automatically picks safe, cost-effective quant profiles per workload bucket while preserving latency SLOs. Design summary...
text: Context: Large tenants with bursty tail traffic waste tokens and GPU cycles during cold KV-cache windows. Goal: introduce a tenant-scoped prefilling heuristic and a quantization-tier manager that automatically picks safe, cost-effective quant profiles per workload bucket while preserving latency SLOs. Design summary: 1) Budget Savant engine: observes recent per-tenant token/latency/cold-start patterns and computes a prefilling budget and prefetch TTL heuristics; 2) Quant Tier Manager: uses a lightweight regret-minimization loop that recommends quantization tier (fp16, int8, or hybrid 4-bit) per model/tenant cohort and ties recommendations to unit-econ delta thresholds; 3) Safe-fallbacks: runtime enforces a safe mode that uses a conservative profile when tail latency deviates beyond configured hysteresis. This PR implements the controller, telemetry hooks, CLI flags for dry-run, and a ...

title: Variant orchestration protocol with adaptive holdpoints and explainable rewind
preview: Motivation: Production rollouts increasingly require fine-grained checkpoints that pause promotion for human or automated validation while preserving deterministic replay and fast recovery. This PR introduces a Variant Orchestration Protocol (VOP) that adds adaptive holdpoints and an explainable rewind mechanism to ...
text:
Motivation: Production rollouts increasingly require fine-grained checkpoints that pause promotion for human or automated validation while preserving deterministic replay and fast recovery. This PR introduces a Variant Orchestration Protocol (VOP) that adds adaptive holdpoints and an explainable rewind mechanism to the rollout runtime.

Summary: Implement VOP primitives, extend the rollout CRD with holdpoints/rewind_policy/priority_succession, add a snapshot-backed rewind store, and wire telemetry anchors into the decision loop.

Key goals: 1) allow staged canaries to pause automatically when a metric drift is detected, 2) enable deterministic rewind-to-checkpoint with an explainability trace for why a rewind was triggered, 3) support priority succession for traffic-lease handoff so higher-priority cohorts can reclaim traffic without full rollback.

Design highlights:
- Holdpoints: de...

title: Holdpoint-carousel phased promote with rehearsal-shadow replay and rollback playbook
preview: Summary: Introduces a holdpoint-driven 'carousel' promote strategy aimed at safe staged promotes across session-sensitive workloads. The feature combines (1) holdpoint anchors — deterministic traffic cut points keyed by session cohorts, (2) a rehearsal shadow runner that replays representative traffic for each holdp...
text:
Summary: Introduces a holdpoint-driven 'carousel' promote strategy aimed at safe staged promotes across session-sensitive workloads. The feature combines (1) holdpoint anchors — deterministic traffic cut points keyed by session cohorts, (2) a rehearsal shadow runner that replays representative traffic for each holdpoint prior to live promotion, and (3) a rollback playbook that encodes automatic and manual abort semantics with quarantine lanes.

Motivation: Multiple incidents where incremental canaries passed aggregate metrics but failed for sticky-session cohorts motivated a promotion strategy that ensures session-coherent rehearsals before traffic cutover. This PR implements the orchestration primitives and a default rollback playbook that operators can tune.

Design:
- Holdpoint controller: computes holdpoints from cohort selectors and session affinity metadata, maintains per-holdpo...

title: introduce-stream-sanitizer-window-and-functioncall-flush-to-preserve-json-span-contiguity
preview: Motivation: Intermittent production incidents and fuzz tests revealed cases where SSE chunk boundaries could split JSON object spans or function-call sentinel tokens in ways that make downstream parsers emit truncated or mis-ordered events. This caused structured-output consumers and OpenAI-compatibility wrappers to...
text: Motivation: Intermittent production incidents and fuzz tests revealed cases where SSE chunk boundaries could split JSON object spans or function-call sentinel tokens in ways that make downstream parsers emit truncated or mis-ordered events. This caused structured-output consumers and OpenAI-compatibility wrappers to drop function-call arguments or mis-associate tokens when partial frames were replayed. This PR introduces a lightweight streaming sanitizer layer and a small reflow window that: 1) keeps a minimal lookback buffer for boundary repair, 2) normalizes delimiter emission order for function/tool-call sentinels, and 3) applies a safe escape-rewrite for split UTF-8/JSON token fragments. The description below summarizes changes, risks, testing, and rollout plan. Changes made: - runtime/sse/stream_window.go: new StreamSanitizer that maintains a sliding reflow window (configurable m...

title: streamline-crosssdk-auth-backoff-telemetry-and-stream-helper-polish
preview: Motivation: Customers and platform integrations reported inconsistent backoff configuration surface and sparse telemetry around auth/reauth flows across the three language SDKs. This made incident triage slower (missing 'auth.retry.count' and inconsistent backoff timings) and increased user friction when wiring stre...
text: Motivation: Customers and platform integrations reported inconsistent backoff configuration surface and sparse telemetry around auth/reauth flows across the three language SDKs. This made incident triage slower (missing 'auth.retry.count' and inconsistent backoff timings) and increased user friction when wiring streaming consumers. This PR harmonizes the public config surface, adds lightweight telemetry knobs, and provides small ergonomic helpers for streaming consumers in each SDK. Scope: 1) Normalize retry/backoff config to a single canonical schema (base_ms, max_ms, factor, jitter) and add parsing shims for legacy formats. 2) Emit normalized telemetry counters/timings for auth handshake, reauth events, and retry backoff windows (auth.retry.count, auth.retry.last_delay_ms, auth.heartbeat.missed). 3) Add an ergonomic StreamingConsumer helper (named StreamingAdapter in TypeScript/Go a...

title: stabilize-sse-terminator-gate-and-ordered-handoff
preview: Motivation: We continue to see production incidents where bursty backpressure and multibyte UTF-8 splits cause mid-json artifacts to be emitted to clients or function-tool call order to be inverted under heavy load. This PR introduces a conservatively scoped runtime change that (1) prevents emission of incomplete JS...
text:
Motivation: We continue to see production incidents where bursty backpressure and multibyte UTF-8 splits cause mid-json artifacts to be emitted to clients or function-tool call order to be inverted under heavy load. This PR introduces a conservatively scoped runtime change that (1) prevents emission of incomplete JSON spans by gating SSE 'data:' events until a terminator check passes, (2) normalizes trailing escape/runout sequences across adapters to avoid truncated UTF-8 and spurious delimiter insertion, and (3) sequences function/tool handoffs with a lightweight ordered-handoff token to preserve toolcall ordering during replay and reconnects.

Change summary: single continuous description of changes made and rationale: Implemented a "terminator gate" in the stream emitter that attaches a compact span anchor to outgoing chunk buffers; the gate delays flush of any buffer that does not...

title: OpenAI toolcall consensus resolver and affordance mapping
preview: Context and motivation: We observed a class of customer bugs where OpenAI-compatible tool calls produced multiple candidate tool invocations or partially overlapping schema outputs in streaming scenarios (e.g., assistant suggests both 'create_ticket' and 'open_case' with overlapping fields). Existing heuristics (fir...
text:
Context and motivation: We observed a class of customer bugs where OpenAI-compatible tool calls produced multiple candidate tool invocations or partially overlapping schema outputs in streaming scenarios (e.g., assistant suggests both 'create_ticket' and 'open_case' with overlapping fields). Existing heuristics (first-wins / highest-confidence) are brittle when tools have similar signatures or when streaming fragments arrive out-of-order. This PR implements a lightweight consensus resolver and an affordance mapping layer that: 1) aggregates partial and final tool outputs during a request, 2) scores candidates by schema affinity, declared affordances, and runtime provenance, and 3) emits a single canonical invoke envelope to downstream backends while preserving a decision provenance header for audit and debugging.

Summary of changes: Implemented consensus resolver in the OpenAI compat...

title: Add token-attribution prober and rollback-suggestion CI adapter
preview: Motivation: We observed regressions that only manifest at the token level (e.g., a previously rare hallucinated sub-token pattern increasing frequency in certain prompt cohorts). Existing cohort-level SLOs catch change in aggregate metrics but not subtle token-attribution shifts. This PR introduces a lightweight tok...
text:
Motivation: We observed regressions that only manifest at the token level (e.g., a previously rare hallucinated sub-token pattern increasing frequency in certain prompt cohorts). Existing cohort-level SLOs catch change in aggregate metrics but not subtle token-attribution shifts. This PR introduces a lightweight token-attribution prober that runs as part of eval-harness smoke checks and a CI adapter that suggests minimally invasive rollback patches when attribution drift exceeds configured thresholds.

Summary: Adds a token-level attribution probe that integrates model attribution signals (attention-based saliency and logit-difference scoring) into the eval pipeline, produces per-prompt per-token drift heatmaps, and new CI logic that: 1) compares attribution fingerprints to historical anchors, 2) raises soft or hard failures depending on severity, and 3) emits rollback suggestions (pr...

title: Adaptive kernel routing and KV-scheduler tweaks for Mistral/Qwen (GQA/MQA tuning)
preview: Motivation: production workloads showed inconsistent latency/throughput tradeoffs across model families (Mistral, Qwen, Llama variants) when using a single kernel selection and KV cache routing policy. This PR introduces an adaptive kernel routing layer and a lightweight KV-scheduler that chooses sharding and prefet...
text: Motivation: production workloads showed inconsistent latency/throughput tradeoffs across model families (Mistral, Qwen, Llama variants) when using a single kernel selection and KV cache routing policy. This PR introduces an adaptive kernel routing layer and a lightweight KV-scheduler that chooses sharding and prefetch priorities per model and request workload, plus targeted GQA/MQA tuning knobs. The goals are: reduce p50/p95 latency variance, increase sustained throughput for mixed-sequence traffic, and avoid tail regressions when switching model variants. Summary of changes (single-line condensed): implement model profiles for kernel selection and quantization-aware algorithms; add KV-scheduler with per-route priority and prefetch window; continuous-batching changes to respect model affinity and early-flush heuristics; small Llama-specific attention correction for 8-bit MQA flows; Qw...

title: Introduce invocation segmentation + weighted-error suppressor and segment-level cost/latency metrics
preview: Motivation: We frequently see noisy error spikes from downstream adapters and third-party integrations that cause alert storming and obscure true regressions. At the same time, token-level cost/latency rollups miss stagewise behavior inside long-running invocations (e.g., prefetch, model-run, post-process). This PR ...
text:
Motivation: We frequently see noisy error spikes from downstream adapters and third-party integrations that cause alert storming and obscure true regressions. At the same time, token-level cost/latency rollups miss stagewise behavior inside long-running invocations (e.g., prefetch, model-run, post-process). This PR adds an invocation segmentation layer that (1) marks logical stages within an invocation, (2) emits segment-scoped metrics (latency, token-cost, errors), and (3) implements a lightweight weighted-error suppressor so transient noisy components don't overwhelm on-call channels.

Summary of changes: added a runtime segmenter used by the serving pipeline, new metrics (invocation_segment_latency_seconds_bucket, invocation_segment_token_cost_cents, invocation_segment_error_weight), tracing span naming for segments (invocation.segment.{name}), log enrichment with segment_id and se...

title: Introduce presubmit cost sampler and toolchain stabilizer
preview: Context: CI noise from transitive dep updates and toolchain version flips has led to intermittent presubmit failures and unpredictable cost spikes in hosted runs. Motivation: reduce noise, gather sample-based cost telemetry, and stabilize the dev toolchain so presubmit failures are actionable rather than transient. ...
text: Context: CI noise from transitive dep updates and toolchain version flips has led to intermittent presubmit failures and unpredictable cost spikes in hosted runs. Motivation: reduce noise, gather sample-based cost telemetry, and stabilize the dev toolchain so presubmit failures are actionable rather than transient. What this PR does: 1) Adds a lightweight presubmit-cost-sampler pipeline (ci/presubmit/sampler.yaml) that runs on ~0.5% of PRs and records job-level time/cpu/mem/infra-cost estimates to the internal CI-telemetry store. 2) Introduces a toolchain-stabilizer service (infra/toolchain-stabilizer/) that: - pins a curated minimal set of tool versions for presubmit lanes, - warms language/OS-specific caches on ephemeral runners, - injects a cache-warm header into existing install steps. 3) Adds an opt-in "quiet-merge-window" gating policy (scripts/merge-window/README.md) that batch...

title: Introduce SSE anchor window and tool-call resume handshake to mitigate partial-JSON replay and delimiter drift
preview: Motivation: Streaming clients have sporadic partial-JSON replay and delimiter drift when network interruptions interleave with server-side tool-call handoffs. This causes downstream parsers to see duplicate delimiters or truncated JSON chunks that are hard to recover without application-level heuristics. This PR int...
text: Motivation: Streaming clients have sporadic partial-JSON replay and delimiter drift when network interruptions interleave with server-side tool-call handoffs. This causes downstream parsers to see duplicate delimiters or truncated JSON chunks that are hard to recover without application-level heuristics. This PR introduces two coordinated mechanisms: 1) an in-stream 'anchor window' that marks a recent stable byte offset snapshot for boundary resynchronization, and 2) a lightweight 'resume handshake' between server and client when resuming mid-stream (used only for OpenAI-compat streaming paths). Design summary: - Anchor window: server emits periodic anchor frames (sse:type:anchor) containing a short stable fingerprint and monotonic offset window. Clients use the last valid anchor to fast-resync when encountering partial JSON (instead of replaying from the start of the message or invok...

title: Heartbeat-driven circuit sanitizer and micro-shed orchestrator
preview: Context: In recent regional incidents we observed long-lived 'open' circuit states in the regional circuit store after transient downstream errors. Those stale-open circuits caused unexpected tenant-level hard-denies during recovery windows and complicated regional failover. This PR introduces a heartbeat-driven rec...
text:
Context: In recent regional incidents we observed long-lived 'open' circuit states in the regional circuit store after transient downstream errors. Those stale-open circuits caused unexpected tenant-level hard-denies during recovery windows and complicated regional failover. This PR introduces a heartbeat-driven reconciliation process (circuit-sanitizer) that periodically verifies circuit health and a lightweight micro-shedding orchestrator that applies tenant-scoped, time-bounded soft-throttles when SLO credit burn is detected. Motivation: reduce tenant-impact from stale circuits and provide a predictable, graceful degradation path that keeps higher-priority traffic flowing while soft-shedding noisy tenants.

What this changes (high level):
- New worker: control/circuit_sanitizer.go implements a heartbeat reconciler that scans regional circuit store, cross-checks active KV cache usag...

title: adaptive-kernel-selector-and-prefetch-degradation-policy
preview: Motivation: recent incidents showed increased p99 latency spikes when the runtime switched kernels aggressively for short sequences and simultaneously attempted speculative KV prefetching. This PR introduces an adaptive kernel selection policy plus a controlled prefetch degradation path that trades a small amount of...
text:
Motivation: recent incidents showed increased p99 latency spikes when the runtime switched kernels aggressively for short sequences and simultaneously attempted speculative KV prefetching. This PR introduces an adaptive kernel selection policy plus a controlled prefetch degradation path that trades a small amount of throughput for deterministic correctness and lower tail latency under mixed workloads.

What this does (single-string description): Adds a KernelSelector that scores candidate kernels by estimated latency, numerical-safety score, and cache-friendliness; introduces a fallback budget token that bounds how often the fast path can be taken per model+device pair; implements a PrefetchDegradationPolicy that steps down speculative KV prefetch aggressiveness when p95/p99 latency or error-rate thresholds are exceeded; exposes a runtime flag --safe-mode to force conservative kernel ...

title: Add ensemble-score-aggregator and CI regression hook for composite eval metrics
preview: Motivation: teams running multi-metric evals need a single, auditable signal for CI gating that reflects weighted regressions across prompt sets and metric families. This PR introduces an "ensemble score" aggregator that computes a weighted composite from a configurable set of metrics (e.g. BLEU, ROUGE-L, exact-matc...
text: Motivation: teams running multi-metric evals need a single, auditable signal for CI gating that reflects weighted regressions across prompt sets and metric families. This PR introduces an "ensemble score" aggregator that computes a weighted composite from a configurable set of metrics (e.g. BLEU, ROUGE-L, exact-match, embedding-sim) and a CI hook that fails builds when the ensemble score or any high-priority submetric regresses past configured thresholds. What changed: added aggregator implementation, config schema, CI integration, unit + integration tests, docs and migration notes. Checklist: [x] implementation, [x] schema & defaults, [x] unit tests, [x] integration test against sample prompt-sets, [x] CI job wired to GitHub Actions, [x] docs and example config in docs/evals. Related: Linear ENG-2141, ENG-2210; references: https://linear.app/redwood/issue/ENG-2141/ensemble-eval-signa...

title: Ephemeral playbook preview + safe-autoreply gateway for low-severity alerts
preview: Motivation: On-call responders spend time drafting and validating small mitigation steps for noisy, low-severity alerts (timeouts, retries, downstream 4xx spikes). This PR introduces two related features to reduce cognitive load and MTTD: (1) an ephemeral playbook preview UI/CLI that generates a short, editable sequ...
text:
Motivation: On-call responders spend time drafting and validating small mitigation steps for noisy, low-severity alerts (timeouts, retries, downstream 4xx spikes). This PR introduces two related features to reduce cognitive load and MTTD: (1) an ephemeral playbook preview UI/CLI that generates a short, editable sequence of suggested remediation steps from the incident context and recent telemetry; (2) a Safe Autoreply Gateway that can optionally send gated automated replies to ticketing/chat channels for low-risk alerts (acknowledgement + recommended self-serve steps), with rate-limiting, audit trails, and kill-switches.

What it does: - Ephemeral playbook preview: incidentctl preview-playbook <alert-id> produces a compact, human-readable playbook draft (3-5 steps) derived from the runbook templates, recent traces, and cached diagnostics. Previews are ephemeral (TTL 24h) and stored en...

title: Entropy-regulated floodgate with failsafe scorer and staged cutover
preview: Motivation: We see intermittent quality regressions after model promotes that are not captured by traditional SLO signals (latency/error). These regressions correlate with increases in output unpredictability for certain prompt slices (high diversity/entropy) which can indicate semantic drift or hallucination spikes...
text:
Motivation: We see intermittent quality regressions after model promotes that are not captured by traditional SLO signals (latency/error). These regressions correlate with increases in output unpredictability for certain prompt slices (high diversity/entropy) which can indicate semantic drift or hallucination spikes. This PR introduces an entropy-regulated floodgate and a composite failsafe scorer to make staged rollouts safer and more automatic.

What this change does: Adds an "entropy" metric (normalized token-level surprisal/diversity) to the rollout decision pipeline, implements a floodgate controller that can throttle promotion traffic when entropy or combined risk exceeds safe thresholds, and a failsafe scorer that synthesizes latency, error-rate, and entropy into a single risk score. The controller supports simulation mode, manual override, and a calibration endpoint. Also incl...

title: Cost Harbor: policy adapter and mini-batcher defaults for tenant-friendly unit-econ
preview: Context: We observed recurring tenant-level unit-econ regressions when autoscaling and batch-routing heuristics chose overly large batch shapes or aggressive quantization tiers for low-SLA tenants. This PR introduces 'Cost Harbor' — a lightweight policy adapter that evaluates tenant budget/latency classes at the gat...
text: Context: We observed recurring tenant-level unit-econ regressions when autoscaling and batch-routing heuristics chose overly large batch shapes or aggressive quantization tiers for low-SLA tenants. This PR introduces 'Cost Harbor' — a lightweight policy adapter that evaluates tenant budget/latency classes at the gateway and applies conservative mini-batcher defaults and quant-tier caps before requests reach the scheduler. Motivation: reduce tail regressions and unexpected unit-cost spikes for cost-sensitive tenants without changing global batcher behavior. Scope: 1) gateway policy adapter service (inline library + small HTTP hook) 2) new tenant-default presets: mini-batcher shapes, max-quant-tier, safe-prefill window 3) CLI preview tool (redwood cost-harbor preview) to dry-run policy decisions against tenant sample traces 4) telemetry hooks and unit-econ regression alerts. Checklist: ...

title: introduce-observability-notebook-and-token-cost-annotator
preview: Motivation: operators need a single, queryable snapshot that ties traces, metrics and token-level cost for a given invocation so oncall and SRE can do fast RCA without stitching multiple services. This PR introduces the Observability Notebook: a minimal server-side snapshot format and a lightweight UI template that ...
text: Motivation: operators need a single, queryable snapshot that ties traces, metrics and token-level cost for a given invocation so oncall and SRE can do fast RCA without stitching multiple services. This PR introduces the Observability Notebook: a minimal server-side snapshot format and a lightweight UI template that surfaces token_cost slices, per-span enrichments and a prompt-shape baseline. The Notebook is backed by a small "cost baseline store" that keeps per-model/prompt-shape median per-token cost for drift detection. What this PR does: 1) adds an invocation snapshot producer (src/observability/notebook.py) that bundles trace spans, token-cost histogram, KV-cache warmup events, and a compact provenance map; 2) enriches spans with token_bucket_label and token_phase tags (pkg/tracing/enrichers/token_annotator.go); 3) emits token_cost.slice metrics (per-invocation slice) and token_co...

title: Autotuned throttle + prefetch coordinator to reduce streaming first-byte latency
preview: Motivation: streaming requests under mixed workloads exhibited spiky first-byte latency (TTFB) and poor tail behavior when KV prefetches and microbatch drains collided with kernel selection. This PR introduces an autotuned coordinator that couples per-stream throttle windows with probabilistic KV prefetch warming an...
text: Motivation: streaming requests under mixed workloads exhibited spiky first-byte latency (TTFB) and poor tail behavior when KV prefetches and microbatch drains collided with kernel selection. This PR introduces an autotuned coordinator that couples per-stream throttle windows with probabilistic KV prefetch warming and drain prioritization, aiming to reduce median and tail TTFB without regressing throughput. Key ideas: 1) Per-stream latency budgets derived from SLA config and recent percentiles drive a soft throttle that caps emission window size and dynamically stretches inter-token yield points. 2) A lightweight prefetch score uses recent KV cache hit/miss ratios, token generation velocity, and kernel warm indicators to trigger prefetches earlier for likely-follow prefixes. 3) Cooperative microbatch drain ordering: streams with tighter budgets and active prefetches are prioritized for...

title: Add predictive concurrency allocator with SLO-gating for canary routing
preview: Motivation: Canary evaluations often surface regressions caused by unexpected concurrency pressure on GPUs and model KV caches. This PR introduces a predictive concurrency allocator that uses short-window time-series features and a lightweight online estimator to predict occupancy and limit per-model concurrency bef...
text: Motivation: Canary evaluations often surface regressions caused by unexpected concurrency pressure on GPUs and model KV caches. This PR introduces a predictive concurrency allocator that uses short-window time-series features and a lightweight online estimator to predict occupancy and limit per-model concurrency before tail latency exceeds SLO thresholds. It ties into the existing canary pipeline so canaries can gate rollouts automatically when predicted saturation is likely. Design summary: - Runtime component: a new allocator service that subscribes to per-model throughput/latency streams and predicts short-term concurrency headroom using exponential smoothing and a small linear regressor. The allocator returns a recommended concurrency cap and a confidence score. - Canary integration: canary scheduler consults the allocator before opening traffic; if predicted tail-latency exceeds ...

title: hackathon-2025-inference-ui-demo
preview: Motivation: lightweight hackathon project to build a minimal interactive demo that visualizes inference internals (batching, KV cache hits/misses, token throughput) and exposes a live-coded frontend that can replay sample requests. This PR introduces a small Next.js front-end, a mock serverless backend that simulate...
text: Motivation: lightweight hackathon project to build a minimal interactive demo that visualizes inference internals (batching, KV cache hits/misses, token throughput) and exposes a live-coded frontend that can replay sample requests. This PR introduces a small Next.js front-end, a mock serverless backend that simulates Redwood's batching behavior for demo purposes, and simple instrumentation endpoints that surface synthetic metrics. The goal is a portable demo that teams can run locally or in a short demo environment to explain inference tradeoffs. Changesets: added frontend pages and components under /demo-ui, a tiny express-compatible demo server under /demo-server, a scripts/serve-demo.sh helper, and a README with run instructions and screenshots. Checklist: - [x] demo UI with token stream playback - [x] mock batching simulator with configurable batch size/latency - [x] instrumentati...
